label_verbatim: A. I hereby disclaim the exclusive right to (describe portion of text or feature disclaimed) — line 1
rationale: First line of the description of the text/feature being disclaimed. Two-line widget composition matches the multi-line convention used on MARK_mark1 (mark.text_words.line{1,2}, etc.).
Second line of the disclaimed-portion description. Widget rect is directly below Text1 (y=632–651 vs Text1 y=655–676), confirming it's a continuation line, not a separate concept.

label_verbatim: B.1. TEXT - list word(s) to be protected, if any (if none, so indicate) — line 1
rationale: First line of word(s) being claimed as protected. Reuses mark.text_words.line1 from MARK_mark1 — the same concept (the mark's text words) appears in both the registration and the disclaimer; the disclaimer just restates them.
Second line of mark.text_words. Widget rect (y=524–544) is directly below Text3 (y=547–566), confirming continuation.

label_verbatim: B.2. FEATURES - describe in detail the design to be protected, if any (if none, so indicate) — line 1
rationale: First line of design features being claimed as protected. Reuses mark.design_features.line1 from MARK_mark1.
Second line of mark.design_features. Widget rect (y=439–458) is directly below Text5 (y=463–483), confirming continuation.

label_verbatim: C. TYPE OF MARK: Trademark - a mark applied to goods applicant manufactures or sells
rationale: Mutually-exclusive enum value 'trademark'. Disclaimer form decomposes the type concept into 5 explicit checkboxes (whereas MARK_mark1 packs type+class into a single free-text widget mark.type_and_class_number). mark.type is therefore a refinement/companion to mark.type_and_class_number; rubric should accept either as the source of type info.

label_verbatim: C. Service Mark - a mark used in connection with services applicant provides
C. Combined Service/Trademark - a mark applied to goods and used in connection with services provided by the applicant
C. Certification Mark - a mark used by an organization to certify the characteristics of goods or services provided by others
C. Collective Mark - a mark used by members of a collective organization in connection with goods or services to indicate membership

rationale: Date the applicant signs the disclaimer.
